steps.title: Read the original circle
steps.content: The equation $(x+2)^2+y^2=9$ has center $(-2,0)$ and radius $3$.

steps.title: Apply both transformations
steps.content: Shifting down $6$ units moves the center to $(-2,-6)$. Doubling the radius gives a new radius of $6$, so $r^2=36=4\cdot9$.

steps.title: Write the new equation
steps.content:
Using center $(-2,-6)$ in circle form gives
$$(x+2)^2+(y+6)^2=(4)(9)$$
The answer is A.
